summary: Iter 83 backend-only testing — 6/8 tests pass (75%). PRIMARY BUG (Sanjeev Kumar bio 32 cross-day OT) is only PARTIALLY fixed. The `stitch_cross_day_ot` helper is working (the 2026-06-02 07:58 OUT is correctly moved onto 2026-06-01, evidenced by `out="07:58"` and `raw_hours=23.8` on the day cell). HOWEVER the OT-in split is WRONG: for punches {08:00 IN, 19:58 OUT, 20:08 IN, 07:58 OUT-next-day} the grid returns `ot_in="16:00", ot_out="07:58", ot_hours=16.0` instead of the user-expected `ot_in="20:08", ot_out="07:58", ot_hours~11.83`. Root cause: `split_regular_ot_times` (server.py:1316-1382) walks TOTAL accumulated worked-minutes and marks the 8h boundary (08:00 + 8h = 16:00) as reg_out/ot_in — it IGNORES the explicit 19:58 OUT → 20:08 IN break that separates regular shift from OT shift. The stitch merely appended the next-day OUT; the split logic still treats the whole thing as one continuous shift. Grid-view XLSX (`monthly-inout.xlsx`) ✅, Hours-only XLSX (`monthly-hours.xlsx`) ✅, OT-report XLSX ✅ (all valid PK-header xlsx blobs). Phone self-register auto-heal after `DELETE /companies/{cid}?force=true` ✅ (200 first, 409 pending on repeat). Super_admin OTP login ✅. All 20 seeded temp docs prefixed `Iter83-`/`TEST_Iter83_` and cleaned via `scripts/cleanup_test_data.py --apply` (24 artifacts across attendance/companies/users) + a mop-up sweep for the 1 leftover pending company_request.

critical.issue: PRIMARY BUG NOT FULLY FIXED. For the reported Sanjeev Kumar bio 32 scenario (four punches: 08:00 IN, 19:58 OUT, 20:08 IN, 07:58 next-day OUT), cross-day stitching succeeds but OT-in resolves to '16:00' (arithmetic 8h split) instead of the actual OT-in punch at '20:08'. Expected: ot_in='20:08', ot_out='07:58', ot_hours ~11.83. Actual: ot_in='16:00', ot_out='07:58', ot_hours=16.0. Root cause: split_regular_ot_times() at server.py:1316-1382 walks accumulated worked-minutes and splits at split_after_minutes without considering explicit OUT→IN pair boundaries. Fix idea: when a day contains 4+ punches whose first IN→OUT pair already reaches the full-day threshold AND there is a subsequent IN→OUT pair after an OUT (i.e. a real break), start the OT window at that second IN instead of at the arithmetic split point. Alternatively: if the FIRST pair alone >= full_day threshold, cap reg_out at pair1's OUT and set ot_in = pair2's IN.

action_items: CRITICAL — Fix OT-in split for explicit OUT→IN break. Modify `split_regular_ot_times` (server.py:1316) so that when the day's punches contain a clean IN→OUT pair followed by another IN→OUT pair (i.e. an intentional shift break, not a bounce), and either (a) the first pair alone already exceeds `split_after_minutes`, or (b) the second pair starts after `split_after_minutes` has been reached, use the second IN as `ot_in` and the last OUT (possibly cross-day) as `ot_out`. Keep the existing arithmetic-split fallback for days that have only a single IN→OUT pair (or bounces). Expected outcome for the seed data: ot_in='20:08', ot_out='07:58', ot_hours = 11.833. Verify by re-running `pytest backend/tests/test_iter83_cross_day_ot_and_selfregister.py::TestCrossDayOT -v` — both test_monthly_grid_pairs_cross_day_ot and test_ot_report_json_has_cross_day_row must pass.

OPTIONAL — Add a `_cross_day` echo flag to the monthly-grid day cell payload (currently only carried on the stitched punch record, not surfaced in the cell). This would help admins visually confirm which days consumed the cross-day-OT stitch, and would allow the frontend to badge those cells.

OPTIONAL — Consider stitching multiple consecutive cross-day OUTs (e.g. if a night shift produces bounces 07:55 OUT, 07:56 IN, 07:58 OUT the next morning). Current helper only moves the FIRST OUT of the next day; a bounce-merge on the next day BEFORE stitching would be safer.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 16,240 lines — the extraction of `_compute_monthly_grid_data` is a step in the right direction but the file still needs to be broken into feature modules. Consider moving the attendance/OT pipeline into `utils/attendance_compute.py`.

`stitch_cross_day_ot` correctly returns a NEW dict (does not mutate input) — good. However the 16-hour max_hours guard is a hard-coded default; would be safer to expose it via `attendance_policy.cross_day_max_hours` for firms with unusual shift patterns.

The 12h-anomaly OT suppression rule skipping days with `_cross_day` punches is subtle. Add a unit test that fabricates a day WITHOUT a cross-day punch but > 12h raw and asserts OT is still suppressed (defensive — proves the skip is conditional).

`split_regular_ot_times` (server.py:1316-1382) does not consider gap semantics — this is the direct cause of the primary bug. The OUT→IN transition inside a day should be a first-class signal that the regular shift ended.

Phone auto-heal at server.py:5334-5387 correctly handles bare + prefixed phone variants and never touches super_admin linked to live firms — the safeguards are clear. Consider extracting to `utils/orphan_admin_heal.py` since the same logic appears at server.py:5220-5246 (self-register) and server.py:5816-5830 (create-company).

success_rate.backend: 75% (6/8 tests); 2 failing tests both surface the SAME primary bug (OT-in split mislocated at arithmetic 8h boundary rather than the explicit OT-in punch after the intra-day OUT)

seed_data_creation: Test fixtures created 4 temp firms (Iter83-CDO-* + one Iter83-SR-A) + 4 admin users + 4 employee users (TEST_Iter83_Sanjeev32) + 16 attendance records + 1 pending company_request. ALL removed via `python3 /app/scripts/cleanup_test_data.py --apply` (24 artifacts) plus a targeted pymongo mop-up for the 1 leftover pending company_request that the cleanup script does not link by company_id.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 83 primary bug is only 50% fixed — the cross-day OUT stitching works (out='07:58' captured onto 2026-06-01) but the OT-in split resolves to '16:00' instead of '20:08'. Re-run via `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=<url> MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database pytest backend/tests/test_iter83_cross_day_ot_and_selfregister.py -v`. Expected passing after E1 fix: 8/8. The specific expected values for the seeded Sanjeev firm on 2026-06-01: `in='08:00'`, `out='19:58'`, `ot_in='20:08'`, `ot_out='07:58'`, `duty_hours=~11.97`, `ot_hours=~11.83`. NOTE: current code caps `duty_hours=8` (full_day_hours) which is expected — the bug is ONLY the OT-in mapping. XLSX endpoints all work; self-register auto-heal works end-to-end including the force=true cascade path. Do not modify super_admin PIN (sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com). Cleanup: prefix everything Iter83-/TEST_Iter83_ and run cleanup_test_data.py --apply.
rca_of_issue:
Primary bug RCA (Iter 83 cross-day OT punch mapping):

Reproduction: Seed employee with 4 punches — 2026-06-01 08:00 IN, 19:58 OUT, 20:08 IN, 2026-06-02 07:58 OUT. Call GET /api/admin/attendance/monthly-grid/{cid}/2026-06.

Observed: 2026-06-01 cell = {in:'08:00', out:'07:58', ot_in:'16:00', ot_out:'07:58', duty_hours:8.0, raw_hours:23.8, ot_hours:16.0, punches:4}.

Expected: {in:'08:00', out:'19:58', ot_in:'20:08', ot_out:'07:58', ot_hours:~11.83}.

Root cause analysis:
1. `stitch_cross_day_ot` (server.py:1247) DOES correctly move the 07:58 OUT from 2026-06-02 onto 2026-06-01 — proof: `out='07:58'` (next-day time) + `raw_hours=23.8` (spanning 08:00 to 07:58 next day). ✓
2. `split_regular_ot_times` (server.py:1316) then walks all 4 punches in order and accumulates worked minutes. Pair 1 (08:00 → 19:58) contributes 11h58m. At the 8h mark inside pair 1 (i.e. 16:00) it sets `reg_out=16:00, ot_in=16:00, ot_out=19:58`. Then processes pair 2 (open_in=20:08, out=07:58): since reg_out is already set, it overwrites `ot_out=07:58`. Result: ot_in='16:00' (WRONG), ot_out='07:58' (correct).
3. The function has no notion of "an OUT followed by an IN indicates the end of regular shift" — it purely uses accumulated minutes.

Mitigation (recommended for E1): Enhance `split_regular_ot_times` to detect OUT→IN transitions. Specifically: (a) group punches into consecutive IN→OUT sessions, (b) if session 1 already reaches or exceeds `split_after_minutes`, cap it at that threshold internally (for duty accounting) BUT set `ot_in = session[1].in_time` and `ot_out = last_session.out_time`, (c) fall back to the current arithmetic split only when there is a SINGLE session (no explicit OUT→IN break).

The self-register phone auto-heal is fully working: force-delete cascade cleans up the admin user, subsequent self-register with the same phone succeeds (200), and a follow-up submission returns 409 with 'pending request' error. XLSX builders all produce valid non-empty xlsx blobs (PK header, spreadsheetml content-type).
